about.seo_description: 19 years of renovation experience in Saint-Louis and the Basel area. Discover the story of Aiman Renovation, our values and our team of skilled craftsmen.
about.hero_subtitle: {experience} years of experience. One goal: to renovate your dreams to the last detail.

story_texts.story: While the others played outside, he was drawing houses. He didn't know it yet, but that school notebook was going to change his life.
Cement under his nails, aching back, 12 hours a day. Nobody gave him anything for free. Every wall built, every pipe welded — he learnt everything the hard way.
There were days when everything fell apart. Lost contracts, closed doors, rain that wouldn't stop. But a man who falls is not a man who's finished. He's a man who's learning.
Team leader. The one you call when things get complicated. 19 years in the field, hundreds of projects, and one certainty: he was ready.
Aiman Renovation. Not just a name on a jacket. A promise. One single point of contact, every trade, zero compromise on quality.
In the heart of the Three Borders region, the boy who drew houses now builds them for real. And he has just one motto: to renovate your dreams to the last detail.

service_items.title: Kitchen renovation
service_items.description: Transform your kitchen into a modern, functional space. From the worktop to the finishing touches, we take care of every aspect of your project.
service_items.longDescription:
The kitchen is the heart of your home — the place where you cook, share and come together. At Aiman Renovation, we design kitchens that combine aesthetics with practicality, tailored to your lifestyle and budget. Whether you want an open-plan kitchen, a central island, or simply to modernise your cabinet fronts and worktop, we support you from start to finish.

Our teams master every trade required: plumbing, electrical work, tiling, joinery, painting. We coordinate the entire project to deliver a turnkey result, so you don't have to manage multiple tradespeople. Every kitchen is designed to measure, taking into account the existing layout, natural light and your daily habits.

We work with well-known suppliers for the quality of their materials: quartz, granite or high-resistance laminate worktops, ceramic or glass splashbacks, durable tap fittings. The result: a kitchen that stands the test of time and adds value to your property.

process.detail: Site survey to record exact dimensions and identify constraints (water supply, electrical, ventilation).
2D/3D layout plan, choice of materials, colours and fixtures together with you.
Careful dismantling, waste removal and preparation of walls and floors.
Plumbing refurbishment, electrical upgrade to standard, tiling and splashback installation.
Cabinet assembly, worktop fitting, connections, painting and final clean.

service_items.whyPro: A kitchen renovation involves plumbing, electrical work, tiling and joinery — all trades that require precise expertise. A faulty seal under the sink, an under-sized electrical circuit or a poorly fitted worktop can lead to costly problems. Hiring a professional means work that meets standards, smooth coordination between trades and a result that lives up to your expectations. Plus, our 10-year guarantee protects you.
service_items.priceRange: €8,000 – €25,000 depending on the area and materials chosen

service_items.title: Bathroom renovation
service_items.description: Create the bathroom of your dreams. Walk-in shower, bathtub, designer basin — we deliver every style.
service_items.longDescription:
Your bathroom should be a wellness space — functional and pleasant every day. Whether you want to replace an ageing bathtub with a walk-in shower, modernise the tiling or completely rethink the layout, Aiman Renovation offers bespoke solutions tailored to your space and budget.

Waterproofing is at the heart of our concerns: we use under-tile waterproofing systems (SPEC) compliant with current French building standards (DTU), guaranteeing a leak-free bathroom for years to come. Our plumbers carry out connections with care, using durable materials (PEX, multilayer).

From wall-mounted basins to double vanity units, large-format tiles to mosaics, we help you choose the fixtures and finishes that match your taste. We also install accessibility solutions (grab bars, level-access showers) for people with reduced mobility.

process.detail: Inspection of existing plumbing, waterproofing check, precise room measurements.
Selection of sanitary ware, tap fittings, tiles and accessories according to your wishes and budget.
Removal of old fixtures, stripping walls and floors, checking substrates.
Hot/cold water network overhaul and drainage, waterproofing system installation (SPEC).
Wall and floor tiling, sanitary ware installation, silicone joints, painting and ventilation.

service_items.whyPro: The bathroom is the most technically demanding room in your home. Poor waterproofing can lead to leaks, mould and serious structural damage. Plumbing must be flawless to avoid leaks. Our craftsmen master DTU standards and under-tile waterproofing techniques. With Aiman Renovation, you benefit from a 10-year guarantee and complete peace of mind.
service_items.priceRange: €6,000 – €18,000 depending on the layout and fixtures

service_items.title: Electrical work
service_items.description: Bringing your electrical system up to standard, full rewiring or extending your installation. Safety and compliance guaranteed.
service_items.longDescription:
An outdated or non-compliant electrical installation poses a major safety risk. In Alsace, many older properties need upgrading to meet NF C 15-100 regulations. Aiman Renovation handles the complete or partial renovation of your electrical system, from the consumer unit to the sockets and lighting.

We carry out a full diagnosis of your existing installation, identify non-compliance issues and propose a tailored renovation plan. Consumer unit replacement, new wiring runs, additional sockets and switches, high-performance LED lighting — every intervention is carried out in compliance with current standards.

We also offer home automation solutions to control your lighting, roller shutters and heating from your smartphone. A smarter, more energy-efficient and more comfortable home.

process.detail: Full inspection of the existing installation, identification of non-compliance issues and risks.
Detailed electrical schematic with positioning of light points, sockets and dedicated circuits.
Running conduits and cables, recessed or surface-mounted depending on the configuration.
Fitting the new consumer unit with differential circuit breakers and surge protection.
Compliance tests, earth verification, commissioning and handover of the electrical schematic.

service_items.whyPro: Electricity does not forgive approximation. A poorly done installation can cause short circuits, fires or electrocution. The NF C 15-100 standard imposes strict rules that only a qualified professional can apply correctly. Furthermore, a compliance certificate (Consuel) is mandatory for major renovations. Entrust your electrical work to Aiman Renovation for a safe and compliant installation.
service_items.priceRange: €3,000 – €12,000 depending on the scope of the renovation (flat or house)

service_items.title: Plumbing
service_items.description: Installation and renovation of your water systems. From pipe replacement to creating new water points.
service_items.longDescription:
Plumbing is the invisible backbone of your home. Old lead or galvanised steel pipes can affect your water quality and cause insidious leaks. Aiman Renovation steps in to renovate, replace or create your supply and drainage networks using modern, durable materials.

We replace old pipes with PEX or multilayer, install thermodynamic or conventional water heaters, and create new water points wherever you need them: utility room, extra WC, summer kitchen. Every intervention is designed to optimise your comfort while reducing your water consumption.

In co-ownerships and individual houses alike, we work on risers, sub-meters and public network connections. Our local knowledge of the Haut-Rhin area allows us to work efficiently with the local water utilities.

process.detail: Visual inspection and pressure test to assess the condition of the existing network.
Detailed quote with choice of materials (PEX, multilayer, copper) and network plan.
Removal of old pipes, installation of the new network with zone isolation valves.
Connecting sanitary fixtures, water heater, washing machine and dishwasher.
Pressure leak tests, flow rate verification and confirmation of no leaks.

service_items.whyPro: A water leak can cause considerable damage in just a few hours: damaged ceilings, mould, short circuits. Plumbing connections require mastery of soldering, crimping techniques and sanitary standards. Choosing Aiman Renovation means a reliable network, clean water and coverage under our 10-year guarantee in the event of a claim.
service_items.priceRange: €2,000 – €10,000 depending on the number of water points and complexity

service_items.title: Landscaping
service_items.description: Design and creation of your outdoor spaces. Terraces, pathways, planting, fencing.
service_items.longDescription:
Your outdoor space is the first impression people get of your home. Well-designed landscaping adds value to your property, extends your living space and provides a pleasant setting all year round. Aiman Renovation designs and creates gardens, terraces and outdoor spaces suited to the Alsatian climate and your personal taste.

Composite decking, pedestal-mounted slabs or natural stone terraces, block-paved pathways, retaining walls, aluminium or timber fencing — we master all materials and techniques. We also integrate outdoor lighting (recessed spots, bollards, LED string lights) so you can enjoy your garden in the evening.

Our designs take account of the terrain (slope, drainage, soil type), aspect and your budget. We plant local species resistant to frost and install automatic irrigation systems where needed. From a freshly sown lawn to flowering borders, your outdoor space comes to life.

process.detail: Soil analysis, slope assessment, natural drainage and sun exposure evaluation.
Project design including terrace, pathway, planting and lighting layout.
Excavation, levelling, geotextile installation and foundation preparation.
Building the terrace, pathways, retaining walls, fencing and gates.
Planting, turfing, lighting installation and clean-up.

service_items.whyPro: Poorly designed outdoor works deteriorate quickly: a terrace that lifts with frost, a pathway that sinks, standing water. Mastery of earthworks, drainage and foundations is essential for a lasting result. Our teams understand the specific challenges of the Alsatian climate (frost, heavy rain) and select appropriate materials. 10-year guarantee included on landscape masonry works.
service_items.priceRange: €5,000 – €30,000 depending on the area and landscaping requirements

service_items.title: Electric vehicle charging point
service_items.description: Installation of electric vehicle charging points. At home or in shared buildings.
service_items.longDescription:
Electric vehicles are becoming mainstream, and with them comes the need for reliable home charging solutions. Aiman Renovation holds the IRVE certification (Electric Vehicle Charging Infrastructure) and installs charging points in individual homes and shared residential buildings in the Haut-Rhin area.

We install wallboxes from 7.4 kW to 22 kW depending on your meter capacity and charging needs. In a private home, installation is generally straightforward: cable run from the consumer unit, charger mounted in the garage or outdoors (IP54). In shared buildings, we handle the technical study, general meeting presentation and individual connection.

IRVE certification is mandatory for the installation of chargers above 3.7 kW and is a condition for accessing financial grants. With Aiman Renovation, you benefit from a certified installer, guaranteed equipment and full support in obtaining the French tax credit (up to €300) and the ADVENIR grant for shared buildings.

process.detail: Checking meter capacity, distance from consumer unit to charger, selecting the right charger type.
Detailed pricing with grant simulation (tax credit, ADVENIR grant).
Running a dedicated line from the consumer unit, fitting the appropriate circuit breaker and differential.
Wall or post mounting, electrical connection, configuration and charge test.
Charger configuration (power, schedules), handover of the IRVE certificate and user guide.

service_items.whyPro: Installing a charging point involves high-power electrics and requires mandatory IRVE certification above 3.7 kW. Inadequate wiring can cause overheating, a tripped breaker or worse, a fire. IRVE certification is also a condition for accessing the tax credit and ADVENIR grant. Take no risks: use a certified installer.

service_items.title: Solar panels
service_items.description: Generate your own electricity. Rooftop solar panel installation.
service_items.longDescription:
The Haut-Rhin region enjoys favourable sunshine for solar energy production, with over 1,700 hours of sun per year. Installing solar panels on your roof means producing your own electricity, reducing your bills and contributing to the energy transition. Aiman Renovation supports you throughout this process, from the feasibility study to commissioning.

We install the latest generation monocrystalline panels, offering optimal efficiency even in cloudy weather. With self-consumption and surplus sold back to EDF OA, you recoup your investment in 8 to 12 years while increasing your property value. For a typical 100 m² house, a 3 kWp installation (8 panels) covers 30 to 50% of your annual consumption.

Grants are numerous and cumulative: self-consumption bonus (up to €1,500 for 3 kWp), EDF OA purchase obligation at a guaranteed rate for 20 years, reduced VAT at 10% for installations under 3 kWp. We handle the complete paperwork and manage the administrative steps (town hall, Enedis, EDF OA).

process.detail: Analysis of your roof orientation and pitch, production calculation and financial simulation.
Prior declaration to the town hall, Enedis connection request, EDF OA contract.
Fitting mounting rails, installing panels and micro-inverters on the roof.
Wiring, AC/DC junction box installation, connection to the consumer unit and Linky meter.
Production test, monitoring activation, handover of the complete technical file.

service_items.whyPro: Solar panel installation involves rooftop work (fall risk) and high-voltage electrical connections. Non-compliant installation can cause roof leaks, fire risk or sub-optimal production. To qualify for grants (self-consumption bonus, reduced VAT), the installation must be carried out by a qualified RGE professional. Aiman Renovation manages the entire project for you.
service_items.priceRange: €7,000 – €15,000 for 3 to 6 kWp (before self-consumption bonus of €1,000 to €2,500)

service_items.title: Outdoor maintenance
service_items.description: Keep your outdoor spaces immaculate all year round. Mowing, trimming, clearing, cleaning — one-off or annual contract.
service_items.longDescription:
A beautiful garden needs maintaining. But between work, family and daily life, who has time to mow every week, trim the hedges twice a year and clean the terrace before summer? Aiman Renovation takes care of the complete maintenance of your outdoor spaces so you can enjoy them without lifting a finger.

We offer one-off interventions — mowing, hedge trimming, brush clearing, leaf collection, anti-moss treatment — but above all, annual contracts. You choose the frequency (weekly, fortnightly or monthly), we schedule the visits and you don't have to think about it any more.

We also have professional hot and cold high-pressure cleaning equipment. Terraces, pathways, facades, roofs — we restore all your property's outdoor surfaces to their former glory. Hot-water cleaning is particularly effective against moss, lichen and ingrained grease stains.

Our contracts include a seasonal review: in spring, we prepare (scarifying, weeding, fertilising); in summer, we maintain; in autumn, we protect (leaf collection, trimming, mulching); in winter, we monitor. It's turnkey peace of mind.

process.detail: We evaluate your grounds: area, vegetation type, current condition, specific needs.
Detailed quote with a tailored maintenance programme: visit frequency, services included, monthly or annual rate.
A visit calendar is set according to the seasons. You are notified in advance of each intervention.
Our teams work according to the schedule: mowing, trimming, cleaning, seasonal treatments.
An annual review to adjust services according to your garden's evolution and your needs.

service_items.whyPro: Regular professional maintenance preserves your property's value and the health of your plants. A poorly trimmed hedge becomes invasive, an unscarified lawn suffocates, an untreated terrace becomes slippery and dangerous. Our teams know the local Alsatian species and adapt care to the seasons. With a contract, you're guaranteed constant monitoring without having to think about it.
service_items.priceRange: From €80 / one-off intervention, annual contract from €150 / month

service_items.title: After-sales and emergency repairs
service_items.description: Water leak, electrical fault, unblocking — we respond 24/7 to secure and repair.
service_items.longDescription:
A water leak at 10 pm, a short circuit on a Sunday, water damage threatening your flat — emergencies don't wait, and neither do we. Aiman Renovation responds 24/7 to secure your home before the damage worsens.

Our service covers all emergencies: plumbing (pipe, tap, cistern, water heater leaks, water damage), electrical (tripped breaker, burnt socket, power outage, short circuit), high-pressure drain unblocking, and anything else requiring rapid intervention. We step in to stop the damage, secure the installations and provide a quote for restoration.

For our existing clients, we also provide after-sales service on projects we've completed. A problem with an installation we fitted? We come back, diagnose and repair. That's what long-term trust looks like.

process.detail: You call us, we assess the situation by phone and give you the first steps to take while waiting.
A technician attends as quickly as possible to secure and stop the damage.
Precise identification of the cause: leak, short circuit, water ingress. Photos and report.
Immediate repair if possible, or temporary safety measures with a quote for permanent restoration.
Scheduling of restoration work if needed. Support with insurance claims.

service_items.whyPro: In an emergency, every minute counts. An untreated leak can cause thousands of euros of damage in just a few hours. A faulty electrical circuit poses a fire risk. Using a qualified professional guarantees a safe, compliant and documented intervention for your insurance. Our local knowledge of Saint-Louis and the Basel area allows us to respond quickly.
service_items.priceRange: Call-out + diagnosis: from €90, repair on quote

service_items.title: Interior & Exterior Painting
service_items.description: Professional painting services in the Haut-Rhin area. Surface preparation, undercoat, and flawless finishes for homes and businesses.
features: Painting on wood, metal and concrete
service_items.longDescription:
Paint is the first sign of a well-maintained interior — and the last item you should cut corners on. At Aiman Renovation, our professional painters work in Saint-Louis and throughout Haut-Rhin to guarantee a durable, uniform and attractive result.

Everything starts with surface preparation — a step that represents 70% of the work and that many amateurs neglect. Filling cracks with smoothing compound, fine sanding, dedusting and applying a bonding primer suited to the surface (plaster, drywall, brick, concrete): without this base, even the best paint blisters in less than 5 years. We use professional-grade paints from Tollens, Sikkens, and Zolpan, in matte, satin or gloss finishes depending on how each room is used.

For damp areas (kitchen, bathroom), we choose antifungal paints resistant to steam. For corridors and staircases subject to shocks, high-resistance alkyd or acrylic paints. For your exteriors, microporous paints that let walls breathe while protecting them from the elements.

We also create decorative finishes: tadelakt plaster for a hammam effect, pearlescent Venetian stucco, wall polished concrete, lime wash for period houses. Whether it is an apartment in Huningue, a house in Bartenheim or an office in Saint-Louis, we guide you from colour selection to final cleaning.

process.detail: Covering floors, furniture and joinery. Masking tape on skirting boards, frames and sockets.
Filling cracks and holes, sanding, washing greasy surfaces, treating stains and mould.
Suitable primer for the substrate to even out absorption and ensure adhesion of the finish coat.
Minimum two finish coats applied by roller, brush or airless spray depending on the surface.
Inspection under raking light, touch-ups of any defects, removal of protection and thorough cleaning.

service_items.whyPro: A successful paint job depends 70% on surface preparation. Without proper sanding, filling and priming, paint blisters, peels and yellows within 2 to 3 years. Our painters master Alsatian substrates (lime render, pebbledash, concrete) and select the right products for each case. Professional tools, flawless finishes, deadlines met — that is the difference between a DIY job and Aiman Renovation.
service_items.priceRange: €20 – €45 / m² for walls and ceilings, preparation and 2 coats included

service_items.title: Flooring & Tiling
service_items.description: Tiling, hardwood flooring, vinyl and polished concrete. Careful substrate preparation and finishing for all interior and exterior floors.
service_items.longDescription:
The floor is the first surface you feel underfoot and one of the elements that defines the style of a room. Large-format porcelain stoneware for a sleek bathroom, oiled oak parquet for a warm living room, polished concrete for a contemporary kitchen — Aiman Renovation lays all types of floor coverings with the craftsmanship that guarantees a lasting result.

Every project starts with a rigorous substrate assessment: flatness check with a laser level, moisture measurement, adhesion test. If the floor is not perfectly level — common in older properties in Haut-Rhin — we carry out a self-levelling compound screed. This invisible step is crucial: tiles laid on a defective substrate will inevitably crack and lift.

For tiling, we master all laying patterns: straight, offset, herringbone, diagonal. We cut large formats with professional tools (diamond tile saw, laser cutting table) for perfectly aligned joints. For parquet, we offer glue-down laying for a solid, silent result, or floating installation for lighter applications.

On terraces and outdoor areas, we lay anti-slip porcelain stoneware (R11-R13) on adjustable pedestals or direct adhesive bonding on a concrete slab. Our work covers Saint-Louis, Huningue, Hegenheim, Kembs, Rixheim, Habsheim and the entire Basel border area.

process.detail: Flatness check with laser level, moisture measurement, identification of structural defects.
Self-levelling screed if needed, bonding primer, crack and expansion joint treatment.
Laying plan defining direction, joint size, starting point and specific patterns.
Glue-down or floating installation depending on material, precise cuts with diamond tools, expansion joints respected.
Matching grout, skirting boards, stainless threshold bars, thorough cleaning and surface protection.

service_items.whyPro: Poorly laid tiles crack, sound hollow and lift within a few years. Substrate preparation, selecting the right adhesive for the format and substrate, and respecting expansion joints all require real expertise. Our tilers use professional tools and bring years of experience in Haut-Rhin. Guaranteed result, compliant with DTU 52.1 and 52.2.
service_items.priceRange: €40 – €120 / m² including laying, depending on covering type and complexity

service_items.title: Complete Renovation
service_items.description: Total renovation of your home from A to Z. One single point of contact coordinating all trades for a turnkey result.
service_items.longDescription:
A full renovation of an apartment or house is a complex project involving a dozen different trades, timelines to coordinate and a budget to manage. Aiman Renovation offers a turnkey solution: a single point of contact who handles everything from demolition to finishes, so you never have to manage the coordination between contractors.

We work on all types of properties: 1970s apartments in Saint-Louis or Mulhouse, Alsatian half-timbered houses in Haut-Rhin villages, modern suburban houses in border towns (Hegenheim, Blotzheim, Kembs, Rixheim, Habsheim). Every full renovation begins with a thorough technical inspection and the drawing up of a detailed project schedule.

Our teams master all required trades: demolition (partition walls, coverings, sanitary ware), electrical standards upgrade NF C 15-100, plumbing (replacement of ageing lead or galvanised steel networks), thermal and acoustic insulation, tiling, parquet, painting, kitchen and bathroom fitting. We work with a network of qualified subcontractors for specialist works (timber frame, roofing, external joinery).

A complete renovation is also the opportunity to rethink the room layout. Removing a partition to open the living room onto the kitchen, creating an extra bathroom, converting the loft — we advise on feasible structural changes and obtain the necessary approvals (prior declaration, building permit if required).

process.detail: Full assessment of the property: structural condition, electrical and sanitary networks, insulation, coverings.
Detailed work programme, layout plans, project schedule and global quote with phased costs.
Removal of old coverings, planned partition demolition, rubble disposal, substrate preparation.
Electrical, plumbing, heating, insulation — carried out in the logical order to avoid rework.
Tiling, flooring, painting, fitting of kitchen and bathroom, site cleaning and key handover.

service_items.whyPro: Managing a full renovation yourself by calling on several separate contractors is a source of stress, budget overruns and extended delays. A contractor waiting on another, clashing schedules, finishes redone because the plaster was not dry — these situations cost time and money. Aiman Renovation coordinates everything. One global quote, one schedule maintained, one ten-year guarantee on all works.
service_items.priceRange: €30,000 – €120,000 depending on floor area, property condition and finish level

service_items.title: Thermal & Acoustic Insulation
service_items.description: External insulation (EWI), internal insulation and acoustic insulation. Reduce your heating bills and improve your comfort.
features: Ground floor insulation on crawl space
Eligible for MaPrimeRénov' and CEE grants
service_items.longDescription:
In Alsace, harsh winters and increasingly hot summers make thermal insulation essential. A poorly insulated house loses up to 30% of its heat through walls, 25% through the roof and 15% through the floor. Aiman Renovation specialises in thermal insulation — whether external (EWI) or internal (IWI) — to significantly reduce your heating bills and improve your daily comfort.

External wall insulation (EWI) is the most effective solution for detached houses: it eliminates cold bridges, protects the building structure from freeze-thaw cycles and beautifies the facade at the same time. We install insulation panels of 80 to 200 mm (graphite polystyrene, rock wool or wood fibre for eco-friendly builds), covered with a render finish in your chosen colour.

Internal wall insulation (IWI) is preferred when EWI is not possible (architectural constraints, listed building, co-ownership). We use glass wool, rock wool or polyurethane cavity wall boards, fixed on metal rails with high-performance plasterboard. Loft insulation, carried out by blowing cellulose wadding or loose mineral wool, is the most cost-effective investment: from €1/m², with a return on investment in under 3 years.

Works eligible for MaPrimeRénov' (up to €75/m² for EWI), Energy Savings Certificates (CEE) and eco-PTZ. Aiman Renovation helps you compile the grant applications to minimise your out-of-pocket costs.

process.detail: Thermal assessment of the property, identification of priority heat-loss areas, energy savings simulation.
EWI, IWI or loft insulation: choosing the optimal solution for the building, budget and available grants.
MaPrimeRénov', CEE and eco-PTZ file preparation. Selection of Acermi-certified materials.
Insulation panel installation, mechanical fixing and bonding, cold bridge treatment, jointing.
Render or plasterboard finish, insulation continuity check, site cleaning.

service_items.whyPro: Poorly executed insulation can create cold bridges, internal condensation and mould, making the situation worse rather than better. Materials must be Acermi-certified and works carried out by an RGE-qualified (Reconnu Garant de l'Environnement) professional to be eligible for financial grants. Aiman Renovation holds RGE accreditation and guides you from the thermal audit to project completion.
service_items.priceRange: €80 – €200 / m² for EWI, €30 – €80 / m² for IWI, €20 – €50 / m² for loft insulation

service_items.title: Facade Rendering & Renovation
service_items.description: Facade rendering, render coats, masonry paint and weatherproofing. Restore your building's appearance while protecting it from the elements.
service_items.longDescription:
The facade is the first impression your home makes. A quality render restores the original appearance of a building while protecting it durably against weather, pollution and UV. In Alsace, the extreme climate (frost in winter, heat in summer, heavy spring rain) demands specific materials and rigorous workmanship.

Aiman Renovation carries out complete facade renovations on detached houses and small apartment blocks throughout Haut-Rhin: Saint-Louis, Huningue, Hegenheim, Bartenheim, Blotzheim, Kembs, Rixheim, Habsheim, Mulhouse. Our teams work on all types of surfaces: monocouche render, traditional lime render, pebbledash, cladding, brick or stone.

Every facade renovation starts with high-pressure washing (150 to 250 bar) to remove ingrained dirt, moss and lichen. Next comes crack repair (filling with fibre-reinforced render, reinforcing mesh on structural cracks), treatment of rising damp and movement joints. The finish render is then applied: scraped, floated, roughcast or sprayed depending on the desired effect, in single or double coat, with a flexible facade coating (RFS) to absorb micro-cracks.

We strictly follow the requirements of the Architects of Historic Buildings for protected areas and obtain the necessary approvals (prior planning declaration) before any work begins. Our operations are secured with certified scaffolding and neighbour protection sheeting.

process.detail: Assessment of render condition, identification of cracks, detached areas and potential water ingress.
Safe scaffolding installation, protection of openings and adjacent green spaces.
High-pressure washing (150-250 bar), scraping of loose areas, brushing and anti-moss treatment.
Crack filling with fibre-reinforced render, reinforcement mesh on active cracks, joint treatment.
Bonding primer, finish render application (scraped, floated or roughcast), colour to your specification.

service_items.whyPro: A poorly executed render can lead to delamination within 2 to 3 years, water ingress into walls and accelerated building deterioration. Erecting scaffolding, choosing renders suited to the substrate and handling structural cracks all require professional expertise. Furthermore, facade works over 20 m² must be declared to the local planning authority. Aiman Renovation handles all formalities and guarantees a result that stands the test of time.
service_items.priceRange: €30 – €80 / m² for a standard render, €60 – €120 / m² for render with repairs

faq.seo_title: Renovation FAQ Basel Area — Frequently Asked Questions
faq.seo_description: Answers to your renovation questions: free quotes, timescales, guarantees, financial grants. Contractor Basel area, Haut-Rhin.
faq.hero_subtitle: Everything you need to know before starting your project.
faq.no_answer: Can't find the answer to your question?

items.question: How do I get a quote?
items.answer: Fill in our online form or call us on 06 33 49 69 25. We will send you a detailed quote within 4 days after the site survey.

items.question: Is the quote free?
items.answer: Yes, all our quotes are free and without obligation. The on-site survey is included — it allows us to assess your project precisely and provide an accurate estimate.

items.question: What does your quote include?
items.answer: Our quote details every item of work: labour, materials, supplies, estimated timescales and payment terms. No surprises: the quoted price is the final price, unless you request changes during the project.

items.question: How long will it take to receive my quote?
items.answer: We commit to sending your quote within 4 days of the site survey. For complex projects (facade, solar panels), allow 5 to 7 working days.

items.question: What is your service area?
items.answer: We work in the Basel tri-border area: Basel, Riehen, Allschwil, Binningen (Switzerland), Lörrach, Weil am Rhein (Germany), Saint-Louis, Huningue, Hésingue, Village-Neuf, Blotzheim, Bartenheim (France), and beyond on request.

items.question: How long does the work take?
items.answer: It depends on the project. As a guide: bathroom 2–3 weeks, kitchen 3–4 weeks, painting a flat 1–2 weeks, facade/EWI 3–6 weeks. The precise schedule is included in the quote.

items.question: Do you work in shared buildings?
items.answer: Yes: facade renovation, insulation, common area refurbishment, collective EV charging installation. We coordinate with the property management company and comply with the building regulations.

items.question: Can I stay in my home during the work?
items.answer: In most cases, yes. We organise the site by zones to minimise disruption. For a full renovation (kitchen + bathroom simultaneously), we can advise on temporary accommodation.

items.question: How does a typical project run?
items.answer: After signing the quote: 1) Planning and ordering materials, 2) Site protection and strip-out, 3) Technical work (plumbing, electrical), 4) Finishing (tiling, painting), 5) Handover with you. A single point of contact keeps you informed at every stage.

items.question: Will the worksite be kept clean?
items.answer: We are committed to maintaining a clean worksite. Protective sheeting is installed from day one, rubble is removed regularly, and a thorough clean is carried out at the end of the work. You get your home back ready to live in.

items.question: What about noise and disruption for neighbours?
items.answer: We observe statutory working hours (8 am–12 pm / 2 pm–6 pm on weekdays). Noisy work (demolition, drilling) is concentrated in defined time slots. In shared buildings, we inform neighbours by letter before work begins.

items.question: Will I have a dedicated contact person?
items.answer: Yes, Aiman personally supervises every project. You have a direct number and can follow the progress of your work in real time. We send regular progress photos by email or WhatsApp.

items.question: What guarantees do you offer?
items.answer: We are covered by professional liability insurance and a 10-year structural guarantee. Specifically: completion guarantee (1 year), equipment guarantee (2 years) and structural guarantee covering major works and waterproofing (10 years).

items.question: Are you IRVE certified?
items.answer: Yes, we hold the IRVE certification (Electric Vehicle Charging Infrastructure), which is mandatory for installing charging points above 3.7 kW. This certification enables you to benefit from the French tax credit and the ADVENIR grant.

items.question: Are your tradespeople qualified?
items.answer: All our tradespeople are qualified and experienced in their field. Our team has 19 years of experience in the building trade. We hold the necessary certifications (IRVE, electrical qualifications) and regularly attend training to stay up to date with standards and techniques.

items.question: What is MaPrimeRénov'?
items.answer: MaPrimeRénov' is a French government grant to fund energy renovation work: insulation, heating, ventilation, solar panels. The amount depends on your income and the type of work. It can cover up to 90% of the cost for modest households. We help you put together the application.

items.question: What are CEE (Energy Savings Certificates)?
items.answer: CEEs are grants paid by energy suppliers (EDF, Engie, TotalEnergies) to encourage energy-saving work. Wall insulation, window replacement, thermodynamic water heater installation: these grants can be combined with MaPrimeRénov' and can amount to several thousand euros.

items.question: Can I get an éco-PTZ?
items.answer: The éco-prêt à taux zéro (éco-PTZ) is a French interest-free loan for energy renovation work, up to €50,000. It is available regardless of income, through your bank. It can be combined with MaPrimeRénov' and CEE grants.

items.question: Is VAT reduced on my work?
items.answer: Yes, for properties over 2 years old in France: 10% VAT for standard renovation work (painting, tiling, plumbing), 5.5% VAT for energy renovation work (insulation, solar panels under 3 kWp). The reduced VAT is applied automatically on our quote.

items.question: Can you help me with grant applications?
items.answer: Absolutely. We guide you through every step: simulating the grants you're entitled to, preparing MaPrimeRénov' and CEE applications, requesting an éco-PTZ from your bank. Our goal is to minimise your out-of-pocket cost.

items.question: What are the payment terms?
items.answer: 30% deposit on signing the quote, then staged payments according to progress (typically at the halfway point), and the balance on handover. We accept bank transfers and cheques.

items.question: Can I spread the payments?
items.answer: Yes, payment is naturally staged according to project progress. For major projects, we can adapt the payment schedule to your situation. The éco-PTZ (interest-free loan) is also an option for spreading the cost without charges.

cgv.seo_title: Terms and Conditions — Aiman Renovation Saint-Louis
cgv.seo_description: Terms and conditions of sale of Aiman Renovation — building renovation works.

sections.title: Article 1 — Purpose and scope
sections.content:
These Terms and Conditions of Sale (hereinafter "T&Cs") apply to all building renovation services carried out by {company}, a simplified joint-stock company (SASU) with share capital as defined in its articles of association, registered with the Trade and Companies Register, with its registered office in {city} (hereinafter "the Company"), for its private or professional clients (hereinafter "the Client").

Any order for works implies unreserved acceptance of these T&Cs, which prevail over any other document of the Client, unless specific conditions are agreed in writing between the parties.

sections.title: Article 2 — Quotes and contract formation
sections.content:
2.1. The Company provides a free, detailed quote following a site survey. The quote specifies the nature and scope of the works, the materials to be used, the estimated completion time and the total price including VAT.

2.2. The quote is valid for thirty (30) days from the date of issue, unless otherwise stated.

2.3. The contract is formed upon signature of the quote by the Client, accompanied by the handwritten annotation "Approved". Return of the signed quote constitutes acceptance of these T&Cs.

2.4. Any amendment to the original quote must be the subject of a written addendum signed by both parties. Additional works not provided for in the original quote shall only be carried out after written acceptance by the Client of the corresponding new price.

sections.title: Article 3 — Prices and revision
sections.content:
3.1. Prices are quoted in euros including all taxes (TTC). The applicable VAT rate is that in force on the date of invoicing (10% for renovation works in properties over 2 years old, 20% in other cases, 5.5% for eligible energy renovation works).

3.2. Prices do not include materials supplied by the Client, unless otherwise stated in the quote.

3.3. In the event of a significant change in the cost of materials or labour between the date of the quote and the start of works (exceeding 5%), the Company reserves the right to revise the price after informing the Client, who has fifteen (15) days to accept or reject the revision.

sections.title: Article 4 — Payment terms
sections.content:
4.1. Unless otherwise agreed, payment terms are as follows:
— Deposit of 30% of the total amount including VAT upon signing the quote;
— Interim payment of 40% at the midpoint of the project, upon presentation of a progress report;
— Balance of 30% upon handover of the works.

4.2. Payments are made by bank transfer, cheque or cash within the legal limit. Card payments are accepted.

4.3. In the event of late payment, late payment penalties shall apply automatically at the statutory rate plus five (5) percentage points, without the need for a reminder. A flat-rate recovery indemnity of forty (40) euros shall also be payable in accordance with Article L.441-10 of the French Commercial Code.

4.4. The Company reserves the right to suspend the works in the event of non-payment of an instalment by its due date, following formal notice that has remained without effect for eight (8) days.

sections.title: Article 5 — Completion times
sections.content:
5.1. The completion times stated in the quote are given as an indication and run from receipt of the deposit and the Client's provision of all elements necessary to commence the works.

5.2. The Company undertakes to inform the Client of any foreseeable delay and its causes. Delays due to force majeure (exceptional weather, material shortages, pandemic, strikes, etc.), modifications requested by the Client or unforeseen discoveries during the works (asbestos, termites, hidden defects) shall not render the Company liable.

5.3. A delay in the completion of works shall not give rise to damages, deductions or order cancellation, except in the case of a manifestly excessive and unjustified delay.

sections.title: Article 6 — Client's obligations
Article 7 — Handover of works
sections.content:
7.1. Upon completion of the works, a handover inspection is carried out jointly by the Company and the Client. A handover report is drawn up and signed by both parties.

7.2. The handover may be:
— Without reservations: the works comply with the quote and professional standards;
— With reservations: defects noted are recorded in the report and the Company undertakes to remedy them within a reasonable time;
— Refused: in the event of major non-compliance rendering the works unfit for purpose.

7.3. In the absence of a handover report, the Client's taking possession of the premises and payment of the balance shall constitute tacit acceptance of the works.

7.4. The handover marks the start of the statutory guarantees (completion guarantee, two-year equipment guarantee, ten-year structural guarantee).

sections.title: Article 8 — Guarantees
sections.content:
8.1. Completion guarantee (Article 1792-6 of the French Civil Code): the Company undertakes to remedy all defects reported by the Client within one year of the handover.

8.2. Two-year equipment guarantee (Article 1792-3 of the French Civil Code): equipment elements that can be separated from the structure are guaranteed for two (2) years from handover.

8.3. Ten-year structural guarantee (Articles 1792 et seq. of the French Civil Code): the Company is liable for ten (10) years for damage compromising the structural integrity of the works or rendering them unfit for purpose. The Company holds ten-year structural liability insurance, details of which are available on request.

8.4. The guarantees do not cover damage resulting from abnormal use, lack of maintenance, modifications made by the Client or a third party, or force majeure.

sections.title: Article 9 — Liability
sections.content:
9.1. The Company is subject to an obligation of result regarding the conformity of the works with the quote and applicable professional standards.

9.2. The Company is covered by professional liability insurance for damage caused to third parties and the Client's property during the execution of the works.

9.3. The Company shall not be held liable for damage resulting from hidden defects in the existing structure that could not be detected during the preliminary site survey, or from materials supplied by the Client.

sections.title: Article 10 — Subcontracting
sections.content: The Company reserves the right to subcontract all or part of the works to qualified subcontractors, in compliance with French Law No. 75-1334 of 31 December 1975 on subcontracting. The Company remains solely responsible to the Client for the proper execution of works entrusted to subcontractors.

sections.title: Article 11 — Termination
sections.content:
11.1. In the event of a serious breach by either party of its obligations, the other party may terminate the contract as of right, thirty (30) days after formal notice by recorded delivery letter that has remained without effect.

11.2. In the event of termination at the Client's initiative, the works completed and materials procured remain payable. The Company shall invoice the works carried out pro rata, plus a termination indemnity of 10% of the value of the remaining works.

11.3. In the event of termination at the Company's initiative, the Client shall be reimbursed for sums paid in respect of works not yet carried out.

sections.title: Article 12 — Personal data protection
sections.content: Personal data collected in the course of the business relationship is processed in accordance with the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) and the French Data Protection Act. The Client has the right to access, rectify, delete and port their data. To exercise these rights, the Client may send a request to {email}.

sections.title: Article 13 — Disputes and applicable law
sections.content:
13.1. These T&Cs are governed by French law.

13.2. In the event of a dispute relating to the performance or interpretation hereof, the parties shall endeavour to reach an amicable resolution. The Client is informed that they may refer the matter to a consumer mediator in accordance with Articles L.612-1 et seq. of the French Consumer Code.

13.3. Failing amicable resolution, the dispute shall be submitted to the competent courts of the jurisdiction in which the Company's registered office is located.

sections.title: Article 14 — Intellectual property
sections.content: Plans, technical studies, sketches and documents provided by the Company in the course of the quote or the execution of works remain its intellectual property. They may not be disclosed to third parties, reproduced or used without the prior written consent of the Company.

article_items.content:
## Bathroom renovation cost in Saint-Louis in 2026

Bathroom renovation is one of the most popular projects in the Haut-Rhin. Costs range widely depending on the existing state and chosen materials. In Saint-Louis, Huningue or Hégenheim, here is what you need to know.

## Price ranges for a full renovation

A complete bathroom renovation in Saint-Louis 68300 typically costs between **€5,000 and €15,000** for a 4 to 8 m² room. This includes:

- Strip-out and waste removal
- Plumbing (water supply and drain replacement)
- Electrical work to NF C 15-100 standard
- Floor and wall tiling (DTU 52.1)
- Walk-in shower or bathtub installation
- Fittings (Grohe, Hansgrohe or equivalent)
- Vanity unit, mirror, accessories
- Moisture-resistant paint or render

## Key cost breakdown

### Plumbing: €800 to €2,500
Many homes in the Saint-Louis area from the 1960s–1980s still have lead or cast-iron pipes that must be replaced. Budget **€1,200 to €1,800** for a full bathroom plumbing refit, with supplies from Cedeo or Point P.

### Tiling: €40 to €90/m² installed
For standard 60x60 tiles laid to DTU 52.1: **€50 to €70/m²**. Large formats (80x80 or 120x60) reach €70–90/m².

### Walk-in shower: €1,500 to €3,500
Very popular in the Saint-Louis area. Price includes the low-profile shower tray, waterproofing membrane, floor drain and wall finish.

### Electrics NF C 15-100: €500 to €1,200
The bathroom is a high-risk electrical zone. A dedicated 30 mA RCD is mandatory. Non-compliance can void your insurance.

## Available grants in 2026

- **MaPrimeAdapt'**: up to 70% of works cost (max €22,000) for adapted bathrooms
- **Tax credit** for home adaptation (elderly/disabled)
- **Anah grant** subject to income

Need a quote for your bathroom in Saint-Louis or the Haut-Rhin? Contact AIMAN RENOVATION — reply within 48h, free no-obligation quote.

- **Mid-range**: €12,000–18,000 (semi-custom units, quartz or granite worktop, glass splashback)

Grohe or Hansgrohe taps sourced from Cedeo or Point P.

Each appliance above 2 kW must have its own circuit breaker. Schneider Electric, Legrand or Hager equipment from Rexel.

R11 anti-slip ceramic or full-body porcelain stoneware.

**By-action scheme** (single type of work): loft, wall, floor insulation, double-flow VMC.

Cumulative with MaPrimeRénov':
- Loft insulation: €250–600/home

€50,000 max, interest-free, up to 20 years, no income requirement, cumulative with other grants.

- Blown mineral wool or cellulose: €25–40/m²
- Rolls: €20–35/m²
- Target thermal resistance: R ≥ 7 m².K/W

### Internal wall insulation

article_items.seoTitle: Old House Renovation Saint-Louis 68300 | Complete Guide
article_items.seoDescription: How to renovate an old house in Saint-Louis 68? Diagnostics, work sequence, budget and pitfalls to avoid. AIMAN RENOVATION craftsman guide.
article_items.excerpt: Renovating an old house in the Haut-Rhin requires method and experience. AIMAN RENOVATION guides you: diagnostics, work sequence and budget.
article_items.author: AIMAN RENOVATION
article_items.content:
## Renovating an old house in Saint-Louis: where to start?

Houses in Saint-Louis, Huningue, Hégenheim or Bartenheim built before 1975 share common features: thick stone walls, timber frames, ageing electrical and plumbing systems, sometimes asbestos or lead. A successful renovation starts with a thorough survey.

## Step 1: Pre-works surveys

- **EPC (Energy Performance Certificate)**: recommended to target priority works
- **Asbestos survey**: mandatory for houses built before July 1997
- **Lead survey (CREP)**: mandatory if built before 1949 with children present
- **Electrical survey**: recommended if installation is over 15 years old
- **Roof structure inspection**: to detect woodworm and damp

## Step 2: The correct work sequence

- **Structure**: foundations, damp walls treatment
- **Roof**: repair before any interior works
- **Insulation and windows**: double glazing, wall insulation
- **Electrical and plumbing**: NF C 15-100 compliance
- **Partitions**: creation or removal
- **Finishes**: tiling, flooring, painting last

Never start decoration if structural works are not completed.

## Alsatian-specific issues

### Damp treatment
Injection treatment: €200–400/ml wall. Moisture-resistant renders: €10–25/m².

### Stone wall insulation
Internal insulation with rock wool lining: €60–100/m².

### Roof structure and covering
Woodworm curative treatment: €30–60/m². Alsatian flat tile re-roofing: €60–120/m².

## Overall budget for 100 m² in Saint-Louis

- Light renovation (surfaces only): €40,000–70,000
- Mid-level (+ electrics, plumbing, partial insulation): €70,000–120,000
- Full renovation (structure, insulation, roof): €120,000–200,000

AIMAN RENOVATION offers a free technical visit and a detailed quote within 48h.

article_items.title: Tiles or parquet: guide to choosing the right flooring in Alsace
article_items.seoTitle: Tiles or Parquet Alsace Haut-Rhin | Guide & Prices 2026
article_items.seoDescription: Tiles or parquet for your home in Alsace? Full comparison: durability, price, maintenance, DTU standards. Craftsman advice from AIMAN RENOVATION.
article_items.excerpt: Tiles or parquet: how to choose the right floor covering for your Alsace home? Durability, price, maintenance comparison and 2026 trends.
article_items.author: AIMAN RENOVATION
article_items.content:
## Tiles or parquet: the great flooring debate

This question comes up in every renovation project in the Haut-Rhin. AIMAN RENOVATION guides you to make the right choice for each room.

## Tiles

### Strengths
- **Durability**: 30–50 years when correctly laid to DTU 52.1
- **Hygiene**: waterproof and easy to clean
- **Water resistance**: essential for bathrooms and kitchens
- **Wide choice**: porcelain stoneware, ceramic, reconstituted marble, cement tiles

### Alsace prices (2026)
- Rectified 60x60 porcelain stoneware: €15–45/m² supply
- Installation + levelling + grouting: €40–65/m²
- **Total installed**: €55–110/m²

## Parquet

### Strengths
- **Warmth and comfort**: superior acoustic insulation
- **Property value**: solid wood parquet increases perceived value
- **Longevity**: solid parquet can be sanded and re-oiled multiple times
- **Design trend**: highly popular in Mulhouse lofts

### Alsace prices (2026)
- Solid parquet (oak, walnut): €50–150/m² supply + €35–60/m² installation
- Engineered parquet: €30–80/m² + €30–50/m² installation
- High-end laminate: €20–45/m² + €25–40/m²
- Oiled oak parquet (Boen, Kahrs): €60–100/m² installed

## Room-by-room guide

- **Bathroom**: Tiles mandatory (waterproofing)
- **Kitchen**: Tiles recommended or protected engineered parquet
- **Living room**: Parquet or large-format tiles
- **Bedroom**: Parquet ideal
- **Hallway**: Tiles (dirt resistance)
- **Stairs**: Solid parquet or R11 anti-slip tiles

## DTU standards

Installation must follow DTU 52.1 (tiles) and DTU 51.1/51.2 (parquet): flatness tolerance 5 mm under a 2 m straightedge.

article_items.seoDescription: Everything you need to know about the NF C 15-100 standard to renovate your home's electrics. Qualified electrician Haut-Rhin 68300.
article_items.excerpt: The NF C 15-100 standard is essential for any electrical renovation. Protection zones, mandatory circuits, consumer unit... Complete guide.
article_items.author: AIMAN RENOVATION
article_items.content:
## The NF C 15-100 standard: the French residential electrical reference

Every electrical installation in a new or renovated home must comply with NF C 15-100 in France. AIMAN RENOVATION explains the key points for your Haut-Rhin renovation.

## The consumer unit

The main distribution board must include:

- A **main circuit breaker** rated to subscribed power (30A, 45A or 60A)
- A **30 mA RCD** (type A or AC)
- **Miniature circuit breakers (MCBs)** per circuit

For a 50–100 m² apartment, NF C 15-100 requires at minimum:
- 2 lighting circuits
- 4 socket circuits
- 1 dedicated washing machine circuit
- 1 dedicated oven/hob circuit
- 1 dedicated dishwasher circuit
- 1–2 bathroom circuits

## Bathroom protection zones

- **Zone 0**: inside the bathtub — no electrical equipment
- **Zone 1**: up to 2.25 m above bath rim — SELV equipment only
- **Zone 2**: 60 cm around Zone 1 — special shaver sockets only
- **Outside zones**: beyond 60 cm — normal sockets and switches allowed

## Cable sizes

- Lighting: 1.5 mm² cable — 10A MCB
- Sockets: 2.5 mm² cable — 16 or 20A MCB
- Dedicated circuits (oven, washer): 4 or 6 mm² cable — 32A MCB
- Bathroom equipotential bonding: 2.5 mm² green-yellow cable

## Common non-compliance in the Haut-Rhin

- Ungrounded sockets (pre-1970 installations)
- Old fuse board with screw fuses
- Undersized cables
- No dedicated circuits for large appliances

Full compliance upgrade for a 70 m² apartment in Saint-Louis: **€3,500–8,000**.

## EV charging point (IRVE)

EV charger installation (IRVE-certified): €800–2,500 supply and fit. Schneider Electric, Hager or Legrand equipment from Rexel.

article_items.seoDescription: When should you replace your home's plumbing? 7 tell-tale signs. AIMAN RENOVATION plumber, Saint-Louis 68300. Free quote.

article_items.author: AIMAN RENOVATION
article_items.content:
## 7 signs your plumbing needs renovation

Plumbing is often the forgotten element of renovations. But ageing pipes can cause costly water damage. In Saint-Louis, Huningue or Bartenheim, here are the 7 signs it's time to act.

## Sign 1: Discoloured or metallic-tasting water

Yellowish water or a rust taste indicates corroded galvanised steel or lead pipes. Many Haut-Rhin homes from the 1950s–70s still have lead pipes. Replacement with **PEX-aluminium multilayer** or **copper** is essential.

## Sign 2: Low water pressure

- Limescale build-up in pipes (Alsace has hard water)
- Heavily corroded steel pipes
- An undetected leak in walls or under the slab

## Sign 3: Persistent damp patches

Damp stains, flaking paint, recurring mould — signs of an undetected micro-leak. A hydraulic pressure test locates it.

## Sign 4: Lead pipes

Banned since 1995. Lead poisoning is a public health hazard, especially for children. Replacement is **urgent**.

## Sign 5: Water heater over 10 years old

Lifespan: 10–15 years. Replace with a heat pump water heater (COP > 3) to cut energy use by 60–70%. Supplies from Cedeo or Point P.

## Sign 6: Constantly replacing tap washers

Too-high pressure (> 3.5 bar) or aggressive water. Pressure reducer (€150–300) and Grohe or Hansgrohe taps.

## Sign 7: Full renovation in progress

If renovating kitchen or bathroom, it's the ideal time to check and replace pipes hidden in walls.

## Plumbing renovation prices in the Haut-Rhin

- Full pipe replacement in 50–70 m² apartment: **€2,500–5,000**
- 200L electric water heater replacement: **€800–1,500**
- Heat pump water heater: €2,500–4,000
- Single tap replacement: **€150–400**

Contact AIMAN RENOVATION for a free plumbing diagnostic in Saint-Louis or the Haut-Rhin.

article_items.content:
## 10 criteria for choosing the right renovation craftsman in the Haut-Rhin

A poor choice can lead to substandard work, budget overruns, or worse — a craftsman who disappears. Here are the 10 criteria to check before signing.

## Criterion 1: Official registration

Every legal craftsman must be registered. Check on **societe.com** or the official French business directory.

## Criterion 2: Ten-year guarantee (Décennale)

10 years of structural damage cover. Request the **valid certificate**. Recognised insurers: Allianz, AXA, Generali, MAAF Pro.

## Criterion 3: Professional liability insurance

Covers damage caused during works. Ask for a separate attestation.

## Criterion 4: Qualifications and labels

- **RGE**: mandatory for energy efficiency grants
- **Qualibat**: construction company qualification
- **IRVE**: for EV charging points

## Criterion 5: References

Ask for photos of similar completed projects and former client contact details to call directly.

## Criterion 6: Clear and detailed quote

A good quote specifies: exact description, quantities (m², ml, units), unit and total price excl./incl. tax, material brands and references, timescales.

Beware of vague lump-sum quotes without detail.

## Criterion 7: Local presence

A craftsman 5 km away will be more responsive. Our teams know local suppliers Point P, Cedeo and Rexel.

## Criterion 8: Communication

Assess responsiveness before signing. A slow communicator before the job will be slow during it too.

## Criterion 9: Deposit rules

- Maximum **30%** deposit on signing for projects under 3 months
- Never full payment before works are complete
- Balance on completion, after inspection

50%+ deposit upfront is a red flag.

## Criterion 10: After-sales service

The craftsman must fix all notified defects within 1 year of completion.

## Why AIMAN RENOVATION?

AIMAN RENOVATION ticks all these boxes: registered, Allianz ten-year insurance, professional liability, detailed quotes, local craftsmen in Saint-Louis 68300. Free quote within 48h.

article_items.author: AIMAN RENOVATION
article_items.content: ## How Much Does a Bathroom Renovation Cost in 2026? The bathroom is one of the most renovated rooms in France. Every year, thousands of homeowners in Haut-Rhin undertake this project. But the question is always the same: how much does it really cost? ## Three Renovation Levels and Their Prices ### Simple Refresh: €3,000 to €6,000 A refresh means giving the room a new look without touching the plumbing. Repainting walls, replacing flooring, changing faucets and vanity. For a 5-6 m² bathroom in Haut-Rhin, expect €3,000 to €4,500. ### Complete Renovation: €8,000 to €15,000 This is the most common project. It includes replacing all sanitary fixtures, plumbing, electrical upgrades to NF C 15-100, and new wall and floor finishes. For a 5-8 m² bathroom, expect €8,000 to €15,000. ### High-End Renovation: €15,000 to €25,000+ Custom Italian shower, built-in thermostatic faucets, natural stone tiles, solid oak vanity, integrated LED lighting. For 8-12 m², expect €18,000 to €25,000+. ## Cost Breakdown by Category ### Sanitary Fixtures: €1,200 to €5,000 A wall-hung Geberit toilet costs €400-800 installed. A complete Italian shower costs €1,500-3,500. A standard acrylic bathtub is €300-600. ### Tiling: €40 to €120 per m² installed Porcelain stoneware 30x60: €40-60/m². Large format 60x120: €55-80/m². Wood or stone imitation: €60-90/m². ### Plumbing: €800 to €3,000 Simple faucet replacement: €200-500. Complete pipe replacement: €1,500-3,000. ### Electrical: €500 to €1,500 NF C 15-100 compliance with safety zones, dedicated circuit breaker, lighting and heated towel rail connection. ### Labour: €35 to €55 per hour In Alsace, qualified craftsmen charge €35-55/hour depending on specialty. Labour represents 40-50% of total budget. ## Financial Aid in 2026 ### MaPrimeAdapt' For accessibility adaptations (walk-in shower, grab bars), up to 50-70% of costs covered, capped at €22,000 for modest households. ### Reduced VAT at 10% For homes over 2 years old, renovation work benefits from 10% VAT instead of 20%. On a €12,000 project, that's about €1,200 saved. ### Éco-PTZ Interest-free loan up to €50,000 over 20 years for energy renovation works. ## FAQ ### What's the minimum budget for a bathroom renovation? For a simple refresh without plumbing changes: minimum €3,000 for 5 m². For a complete renovation: around €8,000 in Haut-Rhin. ### How long do the works take? Simple refresh: 3-5 days. Complete renovation: 2-3 weeks. High-end project: 3-4 weeks. ### Does a bathroom renovation increase property value? Yes, a modern bathroom can increase property value by 3-5%. For a €200,000 apartment in Mulhouse or Saint-Louis, that's €6,000-10,000 in added value. Contact AIMAN RENOVATION for a free, personalized quote.

article_items.seoDescription: External wall insulation in Haut-Rhin: materials, prices, MaPrimeRénov' 2026 aid amounts, and return on investment. Complete guide.
article_items.excerpt: Everything about external wall insulation (EWI) in Haut-Rhin: material choices, price per m², MaPrimeRénov' 2026 aid, CEE, éco-PTZ, and return on investment.
article_items.author: AIMAN RENOVATION
article_items.content: ## External Wall Insulation in Haut-Rhin: Why Now Is the Time Haut-Rhin is one of France's coldest departments in winter. With average January temperatures between -2°C and 3°C in Mulhouse, heating represents a major share of household budgets. A poorly insulated house loses 20-25% of its heat through exterior walls. ## What Is EWI? External wall insulation wraps your home's exterior walls with insulation panels covered by render or cladding. Unlike internal insulation, EWI doesn't reduce living space and works are carried out entirely outside. ## Insulation Materials ### Expanded Polystyrene (EPS): €80-130/m² installed Best price-performance ratio. Thermal conductivity: 0.032-0.038 W/(m·K). 14 cm provides R ≥ 3.7 m²·K/W. ### Rock Wool: €100-160/m² installed Excellent fire resistance (A1 rating) and acoustic performance. More vapour-permeable than EPS. ### Wood Fibre: €120-200/m² installed Best thermal phase shift (8-10 hours vs 4-5 for EPS). Excellent summer comfort. Most environmentally friendly option. ## Haut-Rhin Specificities Continental climate with wide temperature swings requires 16-20 cm insulation thickness (R ≥ 4.5). Pre-1975 houses are most cost-effective to insulate. Half-timbered facades require special treatment. ## MaPrimeRénov' 2026 Amounts ### Per-Measure Path Very modest households: €75/m². Modest: €60/m². Intermediate: €40/m². Upper: €15/m². ### Accompanied Path For 2+ DPE class gain: up to €32,000 for very modest households. Requires certified Renovation Advisor. ## Other Stackable Aid - CEE: €1,500-4,000 - Éco-PTZ: up to €50,000 interest-free - Regional Grand Est aids ## Return on Investment For 100 m² of walls: €10,000-20,000 gross cost. After aid: €3,000-12,000 out of pocket. Annual heating savings: €500-900. Payback: 4-15 years. Plus 5-15% property value increase. ## FAQ ### How much does EWI cost for 100 m² of walls? €10,000-20,000 gross. After MaPrimeRénov' and CEE: €3,000-6,000 for modest households. ### Does EWI require planning permission? No, just a prior declaration (1-month processing). 2 months in protected heritage areas. ### Which material for Haut-Rhin? Wood fibre offers the best year-round comfort. EPS is most economical. Rock wool for fire resistance. Contact AIMAN RENOVATION for a free thermal assessment and quote with aid simulation.

article_items.title: Old House Renovation in the Tri-Border Area: Specificities
article_items.seoTitle: Old House Renovation Tri-Border Area | Guide
article_items.seoDescription: Renovating an old house in the France-Switzerland-Germany tri-border area: local specificities, common issues, and expert solutions.
article_items.excerpt: Half-timbered houses, multi-story homes, Alsatian farms: renovating an old house in the tri-border area requires specific expertise. Complete guide to local specificities.
article_items.author: AIMAN RENOVATION
article_items.content: ## Renovating an Old House in the Tri-Border Area: A Unique Challenge The tri-border area uniting southern Alsace, northwestern Switzerland, and southwestern Germany has an exceptionally rich built heritage. From Huningue's half-timbered houses to Hégenheim's Alsatian farms, from Basel's Altbauten to Markgräflerland village houses. ## Types of Old Houses ### Half-Timbered Houses Timber-frame construction with infill of cob, brick, or sandstone. Found in Saint-Louis, Huningue, Hégenheim, and Blotzheim. Challenges: wood-boring insects, crumbling infill, shallow foundations, rising damp. ### Multi-Story Alsatian Houses (1850-1940) Rubble stone or brick masonry, wooden beam floors, steep-pitched tile roofs. Thick walls (40-60 cm) with good thermal inertia but poor insulation. ### Alsatian Farms Large buildings combining living quarters and agricultural use. Magnificent oak frames, massive volumes, earth or stone floors. Conversion to modern homes is complex but rewarding. ### Basel Altbauten and Markgräflerland Houses Basel's 1880-1920 apartment buildings with high ceilings and decorative stucco. German houses in rendered masonry. ## Regulatory Differences ### France DTU standards, prior declaration for exterior changes. 10-year structural warranty, 1-year defect liability. ### Switzerland SIA standards, cantonal building permits. Stricter energy requirements. SECO declaration for French craftsmen. ### Germany DIN standards, GEG energy regulations. BEG federal renovation aid. ## Common Issues ### Moisture Rising damp (high water table near Rhine), lateral infiltration, interior condensation. Treatment: resin injection (€200-400/linear metre), drainage, VMC installation. ### Insufficient Insulation 50 cm stone walls: R = 0.5-1 vs R = 4-5 for modern standards. Insulation is the most cost-effective investment. Use breathable materials for old walls. ### Weak Foundations Shallow rubble masonry foundations. Signs: staircase cracks, doors not closing, sinking floors. Reinforcement: €5,000-20,000. ## Preserving Character While Modernising Restore original elements rather than replacing. Respect proportions and materials. Use lime renders over cement. Choose biosourced insulation (wood fibre, hemp, cork) for breathability. ## Cross-Border Workers as Clients Thousands of French cross-border workers know Swiss and German quality standards. At AIMAN RENOVATION, we offer evening and Saturday appointments and daily WhatsApp photo updates. ## FAQ ### How much does it cost to renovate a 120 m² old house? Intermediate renovation: €80,000-140,000. Complete renovation including roof and foundations: €150,000-250,000. ### Can a French craftsman work in Switzerland and Germany? Yes. In Germany, EU free movement applies. In Switzerland, SECO declaration required for stays over 8 days/year. ### Interior or exterior insulation for half-timbered houses? Interior with breathable materials for visible timber facades. Exterior for non-visible walls. Mixed approach often best. Contact AIMAN RENOVATION for a free technical visit. We know every municipality and building type in the region.
